internal_links_used.anchor: single fixed rate of $599 per employee per month

plain_text_links_link_gate.label: Hiring in the United States, overview

compliance_cache_used: sprint/compliance-cache/indiana.json (FIRST Indiana page — wrote state_income_tax, county_income_tax, sui, withholding_forms, deposit_cadence, minimum_wage, new_hire_reporting blocks for siblings to reuse). Critical capture: the county-tax overlay across all 92 counties is the most distinctive Indiana payroll feature; the 1 January residence lock via WH-4 is the operational discipline point.
high_concern_flags: factual_uncertainty:prompt_correction — prompt cited Indiana flat rate 3.05% (that was 2024); VERIFIED actual 2026 rate is 2.95% per Indiana DOR Departmental Notice #1, Tax Foundation 2026 tables, Payroll.org bulletin (12 Dec 2025). 2025 was 3.00%; 2027 scheduled at 2.90%. Page uses 2.95% throughout.

factual_uncertainty:medium — specific county rates beyond Marion (2.02%) and Pulaski (3.38%) cited from secondary sources (countrytaxcalc.com, BlueWave HR, Indiana Payroll Guide). Allen/Fort Wayne 1.59%, St. Joseph/South Bend 1.75%, Lake 1.50%, Hamilton 1.10% all flagged 'verify current rate' in table footnotes. Refresh against DOR DN01 December 2025 update before any client-pricing decision.

factual_uncertainty:low — six counties named as having 2026 rate changes (per Payroll.org 12 Dec 2025) cited generically; only Carroll County's specific change (0.022733 → 0.024733) named in the pad-note. Full list of six counties not enumerated due to source 403 on payroll.org WebFetch. Annotate the list during the next DOR DN01 review.

factual_uncertainty:low — WH-1 deposit cadence dollar thresholds (annual / quarterly / monthly / early-filer) not published on the public DOR overview page. Page describes the four-tier structure without naming specific dollar thresholds. Confirm via INTIME account or DOR call before naming thresholds in client documentation.

pullquote_note: Pullquote anonymised to 'Teamed Client Operations' per provenance rule. Subject: the all-92-counties overlay as the most distinctive Indiana payroll operational point. No <cite> tag used. Tom Price-Daniel tagline close stays as brand voice.
voice_notes: v2 editorial standard applied: 4-paragraph capsule (stake → size → ack → promise), no statute refs in body, statutes only in tables (Ind. Code §)
Three named Indiana characters introduced and reused: Caleb (Indianapolis developer), Naomi (Fort Wayne sales lead), Mason (South Bend operations)
H2 ratio: 3 statements + 4 direct questions (43% question rate, within v2 target band)
UK English in body prose (whilst, recognise, behaviour)
No em-dashes in body prose (linter-verified — — entities used throughout)
Answer-first ≤30 words per section, lead with the number
Anonymised Teamed Client Operations pullquote (county-overlay as discipline point)
Single fixed rate / Zero FX / Crossover signal all present in body
Human-first signal: 'named country specialist', 'in-house payroll specialist', 'message the same person', 'no support tickets, no chatbot triage' present
One platform signal: 'one platform', 'contractor through EOR to entity, one timeline, one platform' present
Cost page honesty anchor: 'passes through at cost, itemised on every invoice', 'no hidden fees', 'every line is auditable' present (required for cost/payroll/tax pages)
End-of-page disclaimer aside present above .meta-footer, parchment styling per v2 spec
